title: Tutorial connection to FinnGen BQ tables
text: get_fg_bq_tables() function creates connection FinnGen BigQuery tables provides easy access available tables. minimum required argument environment specifies running queries. Typically, environment sandbox-XX, XX number sandbox using. can find number sandbox URL logged FinnGen Data Access Portal.  Moreover, internal use possible specify ‘build’ ‘preview’ environment.

title: Tutorial connection to FinnGen BQ tables
text: default get_fg_bq_tables() find latest available data freeze table versions can specify specific version needed.
code: fgbq <- get_fg_bq_tables(   environment = "sandbox-XX" ) #> Connecting to BigQuery... #> Using data freeze: dev #> Finding latest table versions... #>   - birth_mother: sandbox_tools_dev.birth_mother_dev_dev #>   - code_prevalence_stratified: sandbox_tools_dev.code_prevalence_stratified_dev_dev #>   - covariates: sandbox_tools_dev.covariates_dev_dev #>   - drug_events: sandbox_tools_dev.drug_events_dev_dev #>   - endpoint_cohorts: sandbox_tools_dev.endpoint_cohorts_dev_dev #>   - fg_codes_info: medical_codes.fg_codes_info_dev #>   - finngen_vnrs: medical_codes.finngen_vnr_dev #>   - hla_imputed: sandbox_tools_dev.hla_imputed_dev_dev #>   - kanta: sandbox_tools_dev.kanta_dev_dev #>   - kidney: sandbox_tools_dev.kidney_dev_dev #>   - minimum_extended: sandbox_tools_dev.minimum_extended_dev_dev #>   - plasma_samples: sandbox_tools_dev.plasma_samples_dev_dev #>   - service_sector_detailed_longitudinal: sandbox_tools_dev.finngen_dev_service_sector_detailed_longitudinal #>   - spirometry: sandbox_tools_dev.spirometry_dev_dev #>   - vaccination: sandbox_tools_dev.vaccination_dev_dev #>   - vision: sandbox_tools_dev.vision_dev_dev #>   - cdm_concept: finngen_omop_dev_dev.concept #> Creating table connections (this may take a moment)... #> Successfully connected to all 17 tables in 7.91 seconds fgbq <- get_fg_bq_tables(   environment = "sandbox-XX",   dataFreeze = "r13" )

title: Tutorial connection to FinnGen BQ tables
text: can join multiple tables together. example, get smoking status asthma patients:
code: fgbq$tbl$service_sector_detailed_longitudinal |>    filter(stringr::str_detect(CODE1, "^J45")) |>    distinct(FINNGENID) |>    left_join(     fgbq$tbl$minimum_extended,      by = "FINNGENID"   ) |>    count(SMOKE2, SMOKE3, sort = TRUE) |>    head()|>    glimpse() #> Rows: ?? #> Columns: 3 #> Database: BigQueryConnection #> Ordered by: desc(n) #> $ SMOKE2 <chr> "no", NA, "no", NA, "no", "yes" #> $ SMOKE3 <chr> NA, NA, "never", "never", "current", NA #> $ n      <int64> 43697, 35806, 32092, 26376, 16633, 15056

title: Tutorial connection to FinnGen BQ tables
text: However, dbplyr sufficient, can still use query() method directly run SQL queries BigQuery tables. example:
code: # Write a custom SQL query sql <- paste0("SELECT FINNGENID, CODE1, SOURCE, APPROX_EVENT_DAY         FROM `", fgbq$tablePaths$service_sector_detailed_longitudinal, "`         WHERE CODE1 = 'J45' AND SOURCE = 'OUTPAT'          LIMIT 5")  # Execute the query result <- fgbq$query(sql)  # Download the results bigrquery::bq_table_download(result) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   FINNGENID  CODE1 SOURCE APPROX_EVENT_DAY #>   <chr>      <chr> <chr>  <date>           #> 1 FG00011698 J45   OUTPAT 2018-08-26       #> 2 FG00012075 J45   OUTPAT 2016-07-12       #> 3 FG00012211 J45   OUTPAT 2021-01-11       #> 4 FG00012223 J45   OUTPAT 2019-12-25       #> 5 FG00012989 J45   OUTPAT 2005-02-13

code: # Access the connection object fgbq$connection  # Access specific fields fgbq$environment fgbq$dataFreeze fgbq$tablePaths

title: Adding Names to Drug Events Data
text: tutorial demonstrates work drug events data using FinnGenUtilsR package. drug events data contains medication purchase prescription records VNR (Finnish national product codes) identifiers. tutorial shows append human-readable medication names ATC codes joining finngen_vnrs reference table. Unlike data types (service sector longitudinal data), custom helper function adding names drug events data. Instead, use simple join operation finngen_vnrs table. understand fgbq object , see dedicated vignette tutorial_fgbq.

title: Adding Names to Drug Events Data
text: add medication names ATC codes drug events data, simply join finngen_vnrs reference table. drug events table contains two VNR columns: MEDICATION_VNR: VNR code actual medication dispensed PRESCRIPTION_VNR: VNR code original prescription (may differ dispensed) can join either column depending medication information need. ’ll join MEDICATION_VNR: Note: VNR codes drug_events table stored strings, finngen_vnrs table integers. need convert type joining. Now can see medication names, ATC codes, information:
code: drug_events_with_names <- drug_events_subject_tbl |>    mutate(MEDICATION_VNR = as.integer(MEDICATION_VNR)) |>    left_join(     fgbq$tbl$finngen_vnrs,     by = c("MEDICATION_VNR" = "VNR")   ) drug_events_with_names |>    dplyr::glimpse() #> Rows: ?? #> Warning: ORDER BY is ignored in subqueries without LIMIT #> ℹ Do you need to move arrange() later in the pipeline or use window_order() title: Adding names to longitudinal and service sector codes
text: previous examples used dplyr syntax fgbq$tbl objects. Alternatively, can work directly BigQuery tables using fg_bq_* family functions, execute SQL queries directly BigQuery. approach useful need control query execution working existing BigQuery table references.

title: Adding names to longitudinal and service sector codes
text: First, need set BigQuery connection table references:
code: library(bigrquery)  # Your BigQuery project ID project_id <- "atlas-development-270609"  # Path to the fg_codes_info table fg_codes_info_table <- "atlas-development-270609.medical_codes_dev.fg_codes_info_dev"  # Create a bq_table reference to your service sector data # This could be a table you've already created in BigQuery service_sector_bq_table <- bq_table(   project = project_id,   dataset = "sandbox_tools_dev",   table = "finngen_dev_service_sector_detailed_longitudinal" )

what: Generate a table in service sector format
title: Adding names to longitudinal and service sector codes
code: # Get events for subject FG00000001 directly from BigQuery ss_subject_1_bq <- bq_project_query(   project_id,   "SELECT * FROM `atlas-development-270609.sandbox_tools_dev.finngen_dev_service_sector_detailed_longitudinal` WHERE FINNGENID = 'FG00000001'" )

title: fg_append_code_info_to_longitudinal_data_sql — fg_append_code_info_to_longitudinal_data_sql
text: longitudinal_data_table full path table longitudinal_data format fg_codes_info_table full path table codes info ICD10fi_map_to INPAT, OUTPAT, PRIM_OUT, DEATH registers ICD10fi combination codes split CODE1, CODE2 CODE3. parameter selects code combination codes map . Options: "CODE1_CODE2" : appends info combined CODE1 CODE2. e.g N0839*E112   (default). "CODE1" : appends info CODE1. e.g N0839 "CODE2" : appends info CODE2. e.g E112 "ATC" : appends info ATC code CODE3 column.  e.g N02BE01 PURCH_map_to PURCH register rows contain VNR code CODE2 column ATC group CODE1. parameter selects two codes append info. "ATC" : appends info ATC code CODE1 column. e.g. N02BE01   (default). "REIMB" :  appends info REIMB code CODE2 column. e.g. 205 "VNR" :  appends info VNR code CODE3 column. e.g. 003121 CANC_map_to CANC register ICDO3  codes split CODE1, CODE2 CODE3. parameter selects code combination codes map . "MORPO_BEH_TOPO" : appends info combined CODE1, CODE2 CODE3. e.g. 8140/3-C61.9  (default). "TOPO" : appends info TOPO code CODE1 column. e.g. C61.9 "MORPO_BEH" : appends info MORPHO code CODE2 column. e.g. 8140/3 REIMB_map_to REIMB register rows contain REIMB code , 1995, also ICD code leaded reimbursement. selects two codes map. "REIMB" : appends info REIMB code CODE1 column  (default). "ICD" : appends info ICD CODE1 column ICD10fi_precision Number leading codes ICD10fi map . e.g. N0839 ICD10fi_precision=3 N08  (default = 5) ICD9fi_precision Number leading codes ICD9fi map . e.g. 8450B ICD9fi_precision=3 845 (default = 5) ICD8fi_precision Number leading codes ICD8fi map . e.g. 36809 ICD8fi_precision=3 368 (default = 5) ATC_precision Number leading codes ATC map . e.g. N02BE01 ATC_precision=3 N02 (default = 7) NCSPfi_precision Number leading codes NOMESCO map . e.g. AB1CB  NCSPfi_precision=2 AB (default = 5) new_colums_sufix string indicating prefix add appended columns, default="".

title: runRegenieStandardPipeline — runRegenieStandardPipeline
text: Submit Regenie unmodifiable standard pipeline via internal standard-pipelines API. Creates phenotype + description files locally, uploads SANDBOX_RED/CO2_temp//<run_tag>/ (using RED_BUCKET gsutil), builds WDL-style input JSON referencing SANDBOX_RED paths, submits workflow. Users allowed add additional covariates using dataframe columns additional covariate.
